skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments
stem: To estimate the mean number of hours per week residents of a town spend on outdoor exercise, Marco interviewed 25 visitors at a local hiking trail. For the 25 visitors surveyed, the mean was 6.8 hours per week. Which of the following statements must be true?

answerOptions.content: The sampling method is flawed and may produce a biased estimate of the mean number of hours per week residents spend on outdoor exercise.
The mean number of hours per week residents spend on outdoor exercise is 6.8.
An estimate of the mean for the town should not be made because the sample size is too small.
The sampling method is not flawed and is likely to produce an unbiased estimate of the mean for the town.

rationale: Marco surveyed people at a hiking trail, who are not representative of all town residents because they self-select into outdoor activity. The sampling method is biased, so the estimate cannot be trusted to reflect the whole population. Choice B treats the sample mean as the population mean. Choice C is incorrect because the issue is bias, not sample size. Choice D contradicts the bias.
